name: DictAny

unions.name: Dict
unions.comment:
A dictionary of keys and values. So a `Dict Key Value` is a dictionary
that lets you look up a unique Key and find the associated Value.

    import DictAny as Dict exposing (Dict)

    type ID
        = ID Int

    comparer : ID -> ID -> Order
    comparer (ID a) (ID b) =
        compare a b

    type alias Museum =
        { name : String, rating : Int }

    museums : Dict ID Museum
    museums =
        [ ( ID 1, { name = "Natural History", rating = 4 } )
        , ( ID 8, { name = "Tate Modern", rating = 3 } )
        , ( ID 42, { name = "Science", rating = 5 } )
        ]
            |> Dict.fromList comparer

    museums |> Dict.get comparer (ID 42)
        --> Just { name = "Science", rating = 5 }

values.name: diff
values.comment: Keep a key-value pair when its key does not appear in the second dictionary.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> DictAny.Dict k a -> DictAny.Dict k b -> DictAny.Dict k a

values.name: empty
values.comment: Create an empty dictionary.
values.type: DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: filter
values.comment: Keep only the key-value pairs that pass the given test.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> v -> Basics.Bool) -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: foldl
values.comment:
Fold over the key-value pairs in a dictionary from lowest key to highest key.

    import DictAny as Dict exposing (Dict)

    type alias User = { age : Int }

    getAges : Dict String User -> List Int
    getAges dict =
        dict |> Dict.foldl consAge []

    consAge : String -> User -> List Int -> List Int
    consAge _ user ages =
        user.age :: ages

    users : Dict String User
    users =
        [ ("Fred", {age=33})
        , ("Bert", {age=19})
        , ("Adam", {age=42})
        ] |> Dict.fromList compare

    getAges users --> [33,19,42]
values.type: (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> DictAny.Dict k v -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment:
Fold over the key-value pairs in a dictionary from highest key to lowest key.

    import DictAny as Dict exposing (Dict)

    type alias User = { age : Int }

    getAges : Dict String User -> List Int
    getAges dict =
        dict |> Dict.foldr consAge []

    consAge : String -> User -> List Int -> List Int
    consAge _ user ages =
        user.age :: ages

    users : Dict String User
    users =
        [ ("Fred", {age=33})
        , ("Bert", {age=19})
        , ("Adam", {age=42})
        ] |> Dict.fromList compare

    getAges users -->  [42,19,33]
values.type: (k -> v -> b -> b) -> b -> DictAny.Dict k v -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Convert an association list into a dictionary.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> List.List ( k, v ) -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: get
values.comment:
Get the value associated with a key. If the key is not found, return
`Nothing`. This is useful when you are not sure if a key will be in the
dictionary.

    import DictAny as Dict exposing (Dict)

    type Animal = Cat | Dog | Mouse

    animalToInt : Animal -> Int
    animalToInt animal =
        case animal of
            Cat -> 0
            Dog -> 1
            Mouse -> 2

    comparer : Animal -> Animal -> Order
    comparer a b = compare (animalToInt a) (animalToInt b)

    animals : Dict Animal String
    animals = [ (Cat, "Tom"), (Mouse, "Jerry") ]
                    |> fromList comparer

    animals |> Dict.get comparer Cat --> Just "Tom"
    animals |> Dict.get comparer Dog --> Nothing
    animals |> Dict.get comparer Mouse --> Just "Jerry"
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> k -> DictAny.Dict k v -> Maybe.Maybe v

values.name: insert
values.comment:
Insert a key-value pair into a dictionary. Replaces value when there is
a collision.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> k -> v -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: intersect
values.comment:
Keep a key-value pair when its key appears in the second dictionary.
Preference is given to values in the first dictionary.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment:
Determine if a dictionary is empty.

    isEmpty empty == True
values.type: DictAny.Dict k v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: keys
values.comment:
Get all of the keys in a dictionary, sorted from lowest to highest.

    keys (fromList compare [ ( 0, "Alice" ), ( 1, "Bob" ) ])
        --> [0,1]
values.type: DictAny.Dict k v -> List.List k

values.name: map
values.comment: Apply a function to all values in a dictionary.
values.type: (k -> a -> b) -> DictAny.Dict k a -> DictAny.Dict k b

values.name: member
values.comment: Determine if a key is in a dictionary.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> k -> DictAny.Dict k v -> Basics.Bool

values.name: merge
values.comment:
The most general way of combining two dictionaries. You provide three
accumulators for when a given key appears:

1.  Only in the left dictionary.
2.  In both dictionaries.
3.  Only in the right dictionary.

You then traverse all the keys from lowest to highest, building up whatever
you want.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> a -> result -> result) -> (k -> a -> b -> result -> result) -> (k -> b -> result -> result) -> DictAny.Dict k a -> DictAny.Dict k b -> result -> result

values.name: partition
values.comment:
Partition a dictionary according to some test. The first dictionary
contains all key-value pairs which passed the test, and the second contains
the pairs that did not.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> (k -> v -> Basics.Bool) -> DictAny.Dict k v -> ( DictAny.Dict k v, DictAny.Dict k v )

values.name: remove
values.comment:
Remove a key-value pair from a dictionary. If the key is not found,
no changes are made.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> k -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a dictionary with one key-value pair.
values.type: k -> v -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: size
values.comment: Determine the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.
values.type: DictAny.Dict k v -> Basics.Int

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert a dictionary into an association list of key-value pairs, sorted by keys.
values.type: DictAny.Dict k v -> List.List ( k, v )

values.name: union
values.comment:
Combine two dictionaries. If there is a collision, preference is given
to the first dictionary.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: update
values.comment: Update the value of a dictionary for a specific key with a given function.
values.type: (k -> k -> Basics.Order) -> k -> (Maybe.Maybe v -> Maybe.Maybe v) -> DictAny.Dict k v -> DictAny.Dict k v

values.name: values
values.comment:
Get all of the values in a dictionary, in the order of their keys.

    values
        (fromList compare [ ( 0, "Alice" ), ( 1, "Bob" ) ])
            --> ["Alice", "Bob"]
values.type: DictAny.Dict k v -> List.List v

name: SetAny

unions.name: Set
unions.comment:
Represents a set of unique values. So `(Set Int)` is a set of integers and
`(Set String)` is a set of strings.

    import SetAny as Set exposing (Set)

    items : Set Item
    items = [ Brush, Soap, Towel ] |> Set.fromList comparer

    type Item
        = Brush
        | Soap
        | Shampoo
        | Towel

    itemToInt : Item -> Int
    itemToInt item =
        case item of
            Brush -> 1
            Soap -> 2
            Shampoo -> 3
            Towel -> 42

    comparer : Item -> Item -> Order
    comparer a b = compare (itemToInt a) (itemToInt b)

    items |> Set.member comparer Shampoo --> False
    items |> Set.member comparer Towel  --> True
    items |> Set.toList --> [ Brush, Soap, Towel ]

values.name: diff
values.comment:
Get the difference between the first set and the second. Keeps values
that do not appear in the second set.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a

values.name: empty
values.comment: Create an empty set.
values.type: SetAny.Set a

values.name: filter
values.comment:
Only keep elements that pass the given test.

    import SetAny as Set exposing (Set)

    numbers : Set Int
    numbers =
        [ -2, -1, 0, 1, 2 ] |> Set.fromList compare

    positives : Set Int
    positives =
        numbers |> Set.filter compare (\x -> x > 0)

    positives --> [1,2] |> Set.fromList compare
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> Basics.Bool) -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a

values.name: foldl
values.comment: Fold over the values in a set, in order from lowest to highest.
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> SetAny.Set a -> b

values.name: foldr
values.comment: Fold over the values in a set, in order from highest to lowest.
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> b -> SetAny.Set a -> b

values.name: fromList
values.comment: Convert a list into a set, removing any duplicates.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> List.List a -> SetAny.Set a

values.name: insert
values.comment: Insert a value into a set.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> a -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a

values.name: intersect
values.comment: Get the intersection of two sets. Keeps values that appear in both sets.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a

values.name: isEmpty
values.comment: Determine if a set is empty.
values.type: SetAny.Set a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: map
values.comment: Map a function onto a set, creating a new set with no duplicates.
values.type: (b -> b -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> b) -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set b

values.name: member
values.comment: Determine if a value is in a set.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> a -> SetAny.Set a -> Basics.Bool

values.name: partition
values.comment:
Create two new sets. The first contains all the elements that passed the
given test, and the second contains all the elements that did not.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> (a -> Basics.Bool) -> SetAny.Set a -> ( SetAny.Set a, SetAny.Set a )

values.name: remove
values.comment: Remove a value from a set. If the value is not found, no changes are made.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> a -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a

values.name: singleton
values.comment: Create a set with one value.
values.type: a -> SetAny.Set a

values.name: size
values.comment: Determine the number of elements in a set.
values.type: SetAny.Set a -> Basics.Int

values.name: toList
values.comment: Convert a set into a list, sorted from lowest to highest.
values.type: SetAny.Set a -> List.List a

values.name: union
values.comment: Get the union of two sets. Keep all values.
values.type: (a -> a -> Basics.Order) -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a -> SetAny.Set a
